rulings.Case Title: ESTHER SHIFFER et al VS. ASBESTOS CORPORATION LIMITED et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Dismiss For Delay In Prosecution
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, September 21, 2017 in Department 503, Line 1. Defendant ABB Inc.'s motion to dismiss for delay in prosecution is granted pursuant to C.C.P. §§ 583.410 and 583.420(a)(1) and CRC 3.1342. Plaintiffs served defendant more than two years after commencing their action for survival and wrongful death. As defendant was substituted in for a Doe defendant, the two-year period began to run on June 30, 2014. Defendant was served more than two years from that date, constituting prima facie grounds for dismissal. Plaintiffs did not meet their burden of submitting a credible excuse for the delay in service. ABB Inc. is dismissed from the action. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:30a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/CML)

rulings.Case Title: THOMAS DUFFY VS. CERTAINTEED CORPORATION ET AL
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, September 21, 2017 in Department 503, Line 2. Defendant Alcatel-Lucent USC Inc.'s motion for summary judgment is off calendar. Defendant was dismissed August 25, 2017. = (503/CML)

rulings.Case Title: THOMAS DUFFY VS. CERTAINTEED CORPORATION ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Defendant Dynalectric Companys Notice Of Motion And Motion For Summary Judgment
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, September 21, 2017 in Department 503, Line 2. Defendant Dynalectric Company's motion for summary judgment is denied. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that plaintiff does not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that he was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. Specifically, defendant failed to demonstrate that plaintiff has no knowledge whether Lera Electric employees exposed him to asbestos-containing joint compound, an exposure identified in plaintiff's responses to special interrogatories but not addressed in defendant's separate statement. This failure precludes defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar v. Atlantic Richfield Co. (2001) 25 Cal.4th 826. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:30a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/CML)
